title: Gentiana meiantha
taxonomy: Gentiana meiantha is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Gentiana, which comprises approximately 400 species of flowering plants. Gentiana meiantha is native to the Himalayan region and is known to thrive in high-altitude environments. The species is often found growing in rocky, moist areas with acidic soil. As a member of the Gentianaceae family, Gentiana meiantha shares characteristics with other gentian species, such as its unique inflorescence and corolla shape.

constituents: Gentiana meiantha is a plant species known for its medicinal properties, but information on its active constituents is limited. intro: Gentiana meiantha is a species of flowering plant in the gentian family, known for its unique, trumpet-shaped flowers and potential medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory infections, and inflammation, due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Gentiana meiantha include iridoids, such as gentiopicroside and amarogentin, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
